back_translation: When a man takes a wife and is her husband, and it shall be, if she does not find favor in his eyes, because he has found in her a shameful matter, then he writes for her a certificate of divorce and gives it in her hand and sends her from his house.
And she shall go out from his house, and she shall go and be to another man.
And the latter man hates her, and writes for her a certificate of divorce and gives it in her hand and sends her from his house, or when the latter man dies who took her to himself as a wife,

then her former husband, who sent her away, shall not be able to return to take her to be to him as a wife, after she has been defiled, for an abomination is she before the Lord; and you shall not bring sin upon the land that the Lord your God is giving to you as an inheritance.

When a man takes a new wife, he shall not go out in the army, and nothing shall pass upon him for any matter; free he shall be for his house one year, and he shall make his wife whom he has taken joyful.
He shall not take in pledge a millstone and an upper millstone, for a life he is taking in pledge.
When a man is found stealing a life from his brothers, from the sons of Israel, and treats him as a slave or sells him, then that thief shall die, and you shall purge the evil from your midst.
Take heed in the plague of leprosy, to keep diligently and to do according to all that the Levitical priests instruct you; as I have commanded them, you shall keep to do.
Remember what the Lord your God did to Miriam on the way when you came out of Egypt.
When you lend to your neighbor a loan of anything, you shall not enter his house to take his pledge.
Outside you shall stand, and the man to whom you are lending shall bring out to you the pledge outside.
And if he is a poor man, you shall not sleep with his pledge.
Returning you shall return to him the pledge when the sun goes down, and he shall sleep in his cloak and bless you, and to you shall be righteousness before the Lord your God.
You shall not oppress a hired hand who is poor and needy, from your brothers or from your foreigner who is in your land in your gates.

In his day you shall give his wages, and the sun shall not go down upon them, because he is poor, and to it he lifts up his soul; and he shall not cry out against you to the Lord, and it shall be in you sin.
Fathers shall not be put to death for sons, and sons shall not be put to death for fathers; each in his own sin shall be put to death.
You shall not turn aside the justice of a foreigner or an orphan, and you shall not take in pledge a widow's garment.
And you shall remember that a slave you were in Egypt, and the Lord your God redeemed you from there. Therefore I am commanding you to do this thing.

When you reap your harvest in your field and forget a sheaf in the field, you shall not return to take it; for the foreigner, for the orphan, and for the widow it shall be, so that the Lord your God may bless you in all the work of your hands.
When you beat your olive tree, you shall not go over again behind you; for the foreigner, for the orphan, and for the widow it shall be.
When you harvest your vineyard, you shall not glean again behind you; for the foreigner, for the orphan, and for the widow it shall be.
And you shall remember that a slave you were in the land of Egypt. Therefore I am commanding you to do this thing.
